oligos.name: Truseq_R2_SBS12_partial
oligos.role: partial TruSeq Read 2 / SBS12 adapter carried on the transposome (i7 side)
oligos.notes: 18 nt; forms the i7/Read-2 stub incorporated at transposition. Completed to the full R2 adapter during i7 PCR.

oligos.name: U-ME (uracil mosaic end)
oligos.role: Tn5 mosaic-end transferred strand with a blocking deoxyuridine
oligos.notes: Written 5'-/ideoxyU/AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'. The 5' deoxyU immediately precedes the 19 nt Tn5 mosaic end and blocks read-through by a uracil-intolerant polymerase, protecting the barcode/adapter on the transferred strand.

oligos.name: SBS12_18_UME_sci_1..96 (transposome loaded oligo)
oligos.role: indexed single-adapter oligo loaded onto Tn5 (transferred strand)

components.name: Tn5_96plx_idx
components.role: 8 nt transposase index (cell barcode, round-1 combinatorial index)

oligos.notes: 96 variants (plate wells A1–H12; Supplementary Table 2), differing only in the 8 nt Tn5 index. Example indices: A1=GAACCGCG, A2=AGGTTATA, H12=ATTGTGAA. Loaded as a single species per well (symmetric tagmentation).

oligos.name: Nextera_Mosaic_End_REVCOMP (ME')
oligos.role: non-transferred / blocking bottom strand of the transposome
oligos.notes: 5'-/5Phos/CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT-3'; 19 nt reverse complement of the mosaic end. Anneals to U-ME to form the double-stranded transposon.

oligos.name: s3 indexed transposome (Tn5 complex)
oligos.role: assembled Tn5 transposome used for tagmentation
oligos.notes: Single-adapter, single-species transposome: only the R2/SBS12+index+U-ME strand is transferred; the 5'-phosphorylated ME' is the non-transferred partner. 96 uniquely indexed complexes assembled at 2.5 µM.

oligos.name: Nextera_R1_A14 / A14_ME LNA (adapter-switching oligo)
oligos.role: LNA/inverted-dT template that appends the Nextera Read-1 (A14) adapter to the i5 side

components.name: U-ME (LNA, 3' blocked)
components.role: LNA mosaic end with 3' inverted-dT block

oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 3. '+N' = locked nucleic acid, /3InvdT/ = 3' inverted dT. LNA raises Tm so it out-competes ME' and anneals to the copied mosaic end; the library strand extends across it to copy the A14 adapter. The inverted dT prevents this oligo itself from being extended.

oligos.name: PCR_i7_P7.S701..S708 / T-series (i7 PCR primer)
oligos.role: i7 indexing PCR primer completing the P7/Read-2 side
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 4 (≥32 variants; e.g. S701 idx=TCGCCTTA, S702=CTAGTACG). Uses NEBNext Q5U so it reads through the residual uracil. The 3' 18 nt (CGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT) match Truseq_R2_SBS12_partial for priming.

oligos.name: PCR_A..H_i5_A..H (i5 PCR primer)
oligos.role: i5 indexing PCR primer completing the P5/Nextera Read-1 side
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 5 (64 variants across plates A–H, wells A–H; e.g. PCR_A_i5_A idx=CCTTAAGA). Primes first in PCR (Nextera i5) and extends through the uracil.

final_library.source_label: s3-ATAC final PCR library (Supplementary Table 1, 'Final PCR product has completed i7 side')
annotation_lines: GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT  full TruSeq Read 2 / SBS12 adapter
[CELL_BARCODE:8]              Tn5 transposase index = cell barcode (read at start of Read 2)
GACGCTGCCGACGA               Nextera Read 1 A14 adapter (reverse complement)

evidence.locator: s3-ATAC final PCR library (Supplementary Table 1, 'Final PCR product has completed i7 side')

library_generation.title: Genomic DNA in nuclei
library_generation.summary: Isolated nuclei present accessible chromatin as the tagmentation substrate.
library_generation.note: Nuclei from frozen human cortex / mouse whole brain are dounce-isolated in NIB-HEPES; ~5,000 nuclei per 96-well tagmentation reaction.

library_generation.title: Single-species indexed tagmentation (SBS-U-ME Tn5)
library_generation.summary: One indexed single-adapter Tn5 transposome tagments the genome, leaving a 9 nt gap and the R2/index/U-ME stub on both ends.
library_generation.note: Each well uses a single uniquely indexed transposome (transferred strand = Truseq_R2_SBS12_partial + Tn5 index + /ideoxyU/mosaic end; blocking strand = /5Phos/ME'). 55 °C, 10 min.

library_generation.title: Gap fill with uracil-sensitive polymerase (NPM)
library_generation.summary: NPM fills the 9 nt gap and copies the mosaic end, but stalls at the deoxyU so the index/adapter stay single-stranded.
library_generation.note: Uracil-intolerant gap-fill closes the top strand up to the mosaic end; the dU blocks read-through into the barcode/forward adapter, preventing premature PCR-able products.

library_generation.title: Adapter switching with A14-LNA-ME oligo
library_generation.summary: The LNA/inverted-dT A14 oligo out-competes ME', anneals to the copied mosaic end, and templates copying of the Nextera A14 adapter onto the top strand.
library_generation.note: NPM extends the top strand across the LNA oligo (10 thermocycles), converting the i5 side to the Nextera Read-1 adapter. The 3' inverted dT keeps the LNA oligo itself unextended.

library_generation.title: Pre-PCR molecule
library_generation.summary: Top strand is now a fully PCR-able molecule with R2/index on one end and A14 on the other; bottom strand remains halted by the inverted dT.
library_generation.note: The reverse (A14/i5) adapter is present as a 5' overhang copy on the top strand; only the top strand can be amplified until PCR.

library_generation.title: Indexing PCR with Q5U (i5 then i7)
library_generation.summary: Uracil-tolerant Q5U PCR: the Nextera i5 primer primes first and reads through the uracil, then the i7 primer completes the P7/R2 side, adding both PCR sample indices.
library_generation.note: 16–18 cycles. i5 primer = P5+i5idx+A14; i7 primer = P7+i7idx+full SBS12/R2. Q5U passes the uracil blockage that halted earlier polymerases.

library_generation.title: Final dual-indexed PCR product
library_generation.summary: Completed library: P7–i7idx–R2–Tn5 cell barcode–ME–insert–ME'–A14–i5idx–P5, ready for standard Illumina sequencing.
library_generation.note: cellID = i5_PCR_idx + i7_PCR_idx + Tn5_idx. Size-selected 100–1,000 bp and sequenced on NextSeq 500 / NovaSeq.

library_sequencing.note: Read 1 primer anneals to the ME'/A14 region on the top strand and reads the open-chromatin genomic insert from the i5/Nextera side.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Nextera Read 1 primer (A14): 5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'

5'- ...[genomic insert] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA [i5rc:8] GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'
                        |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...[genomic insert] GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT [i5:8]   CACATCTAGAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA -5'
       <--------------- GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT
                       (primer binds ME'+A14 on top strand; reads the insert, 85 nt)

library_sequencing.primer: TruSeq index-read-1 primer (anneals to the R2/SBS12 region)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8 nt i7 PCR sample index adjacent to the P7 adapter; run as a 10-cycle index read.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index read (TruSeq index read 1) - primer anneals to the SBS12/R2 region:

5'- ...CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT [i7:8]  GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT... -3'
       ||||||||||||||||||||||||         |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...GTTCGTCTTCTGCCGTATGCTCTA [i7':8] CACTGACCTCAAGTCTGCACACGAGAAGGCTAGA... -5'
                       <--- reads i7 sample index (8 nt), 10-cycle index read

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8 nt i5 PCR sample index adjacent to the P5 adapter; run as a 10-cycle index read.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i5 index read (Nextera index read 2) - primer anneals to the A14 region:

5'- ...GACGCTGCCGACGA [i5:8]  GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'
       ||||||||||||||         |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...CTGCGACGGCTGCT [i5':8] CACATCTAGAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA -5'
       TCGTCGGCAGCGTC ---> reads i5 sample index (8 nt), 10-cycle index read

library_sequencing.note: Read 2 primer anneals to the bottom strand at the SBS12/R2 region and reads the 8 nt Tn5 transposase cell barcode, then the 19 nt mosaic end, then the genomic insert.
library_sequencing.diagram:
TruSeq Read 2 / SBS12 primer:
       GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT --------------->
5'- ...GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT [Tn5BC:8]  AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [insert]... -3'
       ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...CACTGACCTCAAGTCTGCACACGAGAAGGCTAGA [Tn5BC':8] TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC [insert]... -5'
      reads Tn5 cell barcode (8 nt) -> mosaic end (19 nt) -> genomic insert, 85 nt

title: s3-ATAC
description: s3-ATAC (symmetrical strand single-cell combinatorial indexing ATAC-seq) profiles single-cell chromatin accessibility. Nuclei are tagmented in 96 wells with single-adapter, uracil-containing indexed Tn5 transposomes; a uracil-intolerant gap-fill plus an LNA-templated adapter-switch reaction converts every tagmented fragment (not just the 50% that get two different adapters) into a dual-adapter, PCR-able molecule, boosting usable reads/cell 6–13× over other scATAC methods. Reads capture the open-chromatin genomic insert plus three indices (a transposase/Tn5 cell barcode read at the start of Read 2, and i7/i5 PCR sample indices) whose concatenation uniquely labels each cell.

throughput.summary: 2,175 human cortex + 837 mouse whole-brain single-cell ATAC-seq profiles across 4 PCR plates; median unique reads per cell ~100,280–178,069 (mean up to ~258,859). 6–13-fold more usable reads/cell than snATAC, 10x scATAC and dscATAC.
throughput.cells: 3,012 s3-ATAC single cells passing QC (2,175 human, 837 mouse); scalable to 10^4–10^5 cells by expanding transposome/PCR index sets
throughput.dna: median ~100k–178k unique passing reads per cell; TSS enrichment 13.4 (human)/13.5 (mouse); FRiP ~31.95%/29.15%

publication.statistical_model: Latent Dirichlet allocation topic modeling (cisTopic) of the peak-by-cell accessibility matrix for dimensionality reduction/clustering; Welch's two-sample t-test for cross-method read-count comparisons; Fisher's exact (hypergeometric) test for marker gene-set enrichment.

other.label: Core innovation
other.value: Uracil-based adapter switching ('s3') converts >50% of tagmented fragments into dual-adapter libraries via a uracil-block + LNA-templated extension, overcoming the 50% yield limit of standard tagmentation.

other.label: Cross-cell collision rate
other.value: 5.53% pre-tagmentation mixing; 0% post-tagmentation (no crosstalk during adapter switching or PCR).

other.label: Compatible variants
other.value: Same chemistry extended to s3-WGS (148-fold usable-read improvement vs sci-DNA-seq) and s3-GCC whole-genome + chromatin conformation (14.8-fold vs sci-HiC).

data_processing.summary: Paired-end reads are demultiplexed by concatenating the Tn5 transposase index with the i7/i5 PCR indices into a per-cell barcode (cellID), aligned to a hg38/mm10 hybrid reference, deduplicated, species-assigned (barnyard), then reduced to a peak-by-cell accessibility matrix that is factorized by LDA topic modeling (cisTopic) and clustered for cell-type discovery.
data_processing.statistical_model: Latent Dirichlet allocation topic modeling (cisTopic); Fisher's exact / hypergeometric test for marker gene-set enrichment; Welch's two-sample t-test for cross-method read-count comparisons
